metadata.description: Book-production skills for writing, structuring and typesetting academic textbooks and monographs in LaTeX — the form/production side of a book project. Experimental, shared as-is with no warranty or liability. Responsibility rests entirely with whoever runs the skill — research integrity, institutional AI policy, publisher contract, copyright clearance, GDPR and the accuracy of every citation stay yours; the developer carries none of it and gives no assurance about your work. Everything runs locally; the developer receives nothing. Source verification assumes institutional library access for full-text checks (written around a CBS login; substitutable with your own university's access).

plugins.name: academic-book
plugins.description: Structure, number, build and typeset an academic textbook or monograph in LaTeX — chapter templates, box/case numbering, front/back matter, cover and print-quality checklists.

plugins.name: faglig-bog
plugins.description: Dansk søster til academic-book: struktur, nummerering, LaTeX-build og trykklargøring af en faglig lærebog eller monografi.

plugins.name: pm-konsistens-audit
plugins.description: Consistency and reference audit for a LaTeX textbook: consecutive numbering per chapter, dangling cross-references, concept-register/appendix pointers, references.bib integrity and typeless box-pointers.

plugins.name: academic-source-verification
plugins.description: Three-check citation verification (metadata/DOI plus content) and a reference-ledger builder for academic manuscripts — flags unverifiable sources, never fabricates references. The claim-fidelity check reads source full text and is written around a CBS-login backend; substitute your own institutional library access (EZproxy/OpenAthens/Shibboleth). Metadata and retraction checks need no login. Experimental, no warranty — verify every result yourself.

plugins.name: pm-bog
plugins.description: Opt-in project skill for one specific textbook (CBS Performance Management) — chapter map, LaTeX build, box definitions and known-good baseline facts. Published for reproducibility, not reuse: it will not be useful for your book. Invoke by name only.
